danger_signals.visual_form: Alien creatures positioned in level space
danger_signals.meaning: Hazards that damage Cosmo on contact or via projectile fire; player must avoid or destroy

danger_signals.visual_form: Visible projectiles launched from enemy entities
danger_signals.meaning: Ranged threat requiring movement evasion or shield protection

danger_signals.visual_form: Bottomless pit gaps in level geometry
danger_signals.meaning: Instant level restart on contact; critical spatial hazard

danger_signals.visual_form: Health bar UI indicating remaining units (3 base, max 5 with cheeseburgers)
danger_signals.meaning: Visual countdown to failure state; each enemy/hazard contact removes one unit
Area damage hazard; can harm Cosmo if proximity too close at detonation

safety_signals.visual_form: Collectible objects restoring one health unit
safety_signals.meaning: Damage mitigation; distributed throughout levels as resource placement
Reward for exploration; permanent health meter expansion (adds empty unit, max 5)
Temporary invincibility shield; negates next damage instance
Safe completion state; progression to next level
Safe traversal mechanic; avoids ground-based hazards temporarily

safety_signals.visual_form: Climbable walls using suction-cup hand mechanic
safety_signals.meaning: Safe vertical navigation; avoids enemy/hazard areas

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Alien boy character sprite with distinctive appearance
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Designated level starting positions; story framing as explorer rescuing missing parents

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Distinct hostile entity sprites per level/episode
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Different creature designs across three episodes reflecting alien-themed level progression
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Placed throughout alien planet environment; indicate territory control/ecosystem

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Large creature that swallows Cosmo at end of Episode 1
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Mega-scale entity indicating apex threat/environmental hazard
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Environment itself becomes hostile; interior of creature is navigable level space in Episode 2

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Alien planet surface environment; diverse enemy types; outdoor/natural structures
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cosmo explores defensively; enemies patrol/attack; story event triggers at level exit (swallowed by creature)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Organic, internal environment aesthetic replacing external alien landscape
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cosmo navigates internal anatomy; must find escape route; enemy/hazard types reflect internal ecosystem

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Urban alien architecture; city environment distinct from natural planet
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Cosmo approaches final objective; convergence of story elements; parents located safely (narrative twist)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Health meter UI decreases visually; Cosmo may flash/change sprite state
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must seek power-ups or avoid further contact; moment of vulnerability

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temporary visual effect around Cosmo entity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: One enemy/hazard contact negated; shield consumed; invincibility state ends

environmental_storytelling.example: Three episodes with 10 levels each; progression communicates story beats: exploration → internal journey → rescue/discovery
Distinct visual design of alien planet, internal creature, and city environment creates narrative momentum without dialogue
Large footprints in story setup suggest predatory inhabitants; later encounter with swallowing creature validates environmental danger signals
Star collection gates access to bonus levels; resource scarcity communicates progression hierarchy
Two cheeseburgers per episode hidden in level geometry; incentivizes non-linear exploration; permanent stat reward signals meaningful discovery

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Standard movement, jumping, wall-clinging, bomb-throwing capabilities available; no restrictions
Same mechanical capabilities but visual/audio feedback indicates vulnerability; behavior unchanged but player risk assessment increases
Continues normal movement/actions; next damage absorbed by shield effect; behavioral change is protection mechanism only
Patrol patterns, static positions, or simple AI routines; no aggression
Attack via contact or projectile fire; pursuit behavior if capable; indicates threat escalation
Removal from level; eliminated threat; resource/point reward
Display helpful message on contact; non-hostile information source
Removed from level; point reward (12,800 points); trades information for score

notes: Cosmo's Cosmic Adventure uses minimal narrative exposition; world-state communication relies heavily on visual environment shifts across episodes and sprite-based enemy/hazard visibility. The 16-color EGA 320×200 resolution constrains visual communication to iconic design—alien creatures must be instantly recognizable as threats, collectibles as benefits, and level geometry as navigable/hazardous. Parallax scrolling reinforces spatial depth, aiding player threat assessment at distance. Health meter is central UI for danger communication; invincibility shield state likely uses color/animation overlay. Bomb mechanics introduce delayed hazard communication (explosion delay forces planning). Story progression (three episodes with distinct visual environments) serves as macro-level world-state indicator without dialogue or cutscenes.
